Artyk India — Site Development

Website ChangesSummary & Changelog

A complete before-and-after record — with screenshots of every change — made to the Artyk India website during this working session: navigation, editorial copy, two rebuilt pages, brand data, interaction design, and a full responsiveness pass.

Date
20 July 2026
Pages touched
5
Changes logged
20

01

Navbar — Squared Geometry

Sitewide

All curvature removed from the floating navigation bar — the outer container and both of its buttons now read as sharp rectangles. Glass blur, shadow and background finish untouched.

✕ Before

Container: rounded-2xl

“By Appointment” button: rounded-full

Mobile “Menu” button: rounded-full

✓ After

Container: no radius — square corners

“By Appointment” button: square

Mobile “Menu” button: square

02

Navbar — Logo Replacement

Sitewide

The navbar mark was swapped for the newly supplied logo file, with its intrinsic dimensions updated so the aspect ratio is exact and no distortion occurs.

✕ Before

artyk-logo-cropped.png

Declared size: 599 × 193

✓ After

artyk-logo-2.png

Declared size: 953 × 262 (true native)

Rendered height is unchanged (h-6 mobile → h-8 desktop); only the source file and its declared proportions changed. The comparison above shows both this and the squared geometry.
03

Footer — Logo Scale

Sitewide

The footer mark was reduced so it sits more quietly against the address and navigation columns beside it. Applies to every page, as the footer is a single shared component.

✕ Before

h-16 md:h-20 — 64px mobile → 80px desktop

✓ After

h-12 md:h-14 — 48px mobile → 56px desktop (≈30% smaller)

04

“The Artyk Difference” — Imagery

Homepage

The four editorial images in this block were squared off to match the navbar’s new geometry.

✕ Before

Image wrapper carried rounded-md — soft 6px corners.

✓ After

Radius removed — sharp, square crops. Drop shadow and hover behaviour retained.

05

“A World of Exceptional Design” — Brand Cards

Homepage & Brands

The horizontally scrolling partner-house cards were squared off, completing the sitewide shift away from rounded geometry.

✕ Before

rounded-[1.5rem] — heavily rounded 24px corners.

✓ After

Radius removed — sharp-cornered cards. Hairline border, gradient scrim and lift-on-hover unchanged.

06

About — “The House” Copy

About

Both the display lead and the supporting column were replaced with client-supplied copy.

✕ Before

“Artyk was born from a passion for exceptional design — and a belief that the spaces we inhabit should inspire the way we live.”

✓ After

“Artyk was created with a singular vision — to curate the world’s finest design and transform the way people experience their spaces.”

The corten italic emphasis was carried across from “inspire” to “transform”, preserving the original typographic treatment.
07

About — Founders Quote & Story

About

The scroll-scrubbed pull quote and the founders’ narrative below it were both replaced.

✕ Before

“Design is not a luxury — it is a way of enhancing how we live, work, and experience the world around us.”

Three paragraphs of story beneath.

✓ After

“Every remarkable interior begins with a conversation, not a catalogue.”

Four paragraphs: founding vision, the founders’ distinct expertise, the destination they envisioned, and today’s portfolio.

08

About — Testimonials → “Our Philosophy”

About

The placeholder testimonials block was removed entirely and rebuilt as a dimensional centrepiece — the visual highlight of the page.

✕ Before

Three sample client quotes with circular stock headshots alternating left/right. Placeholder names and roles throughout.

✓ After

“Thoughtfully Chosen. Beautifully Experienced.” on a floating plaque in true CSS 3D perspective, with the client’s three paragraphs beneath.

The interaction: the plaque leans toward the cursor with sprung inertia; heading floats 54px above the surface and copy at 30px so tilting produces real parallax; a specular sheen travels with the pointer; letterpress heading, rotating jewel ornament and a double hairline frame. On touch it turns gently on its own, and prefers-reduced-motion resolves everything to a static state.
09

About — Five New Editorial Chapters

About

Everything after “Our Philosophy” was rebuilt as a sequence of storytelling chapters. The previous plain index rows and closing block were retired, their content superseded.

✕ Before

“What Sets Us Apart” — four flat text rows in a bordered list.

Closing — a single two-column block.

✓ After

Vision · Mission · a sticky “private exhibition” · a museum-wall Values sequence · a full-viewport finale.

Finale — “An Address for Design.”

Bug found & fixed during build: Chrome computes IntersectionObserver geometry after clip-path, so a fully-clipped image never reports as visible and its reveal never fires. Reveals now observe the unclipped wrapper and drive the clip from there.
10

About — Vision Frame Collision

About

On the Vision chapter, the decorative offset frame was enclosing the caption text, causing a visible collision. Reported from review and corrected.

✕ Before

The frame was positioned against the whole figure — image and caption — so its offset trapped the caption inside the frame, crossing its borders.

✓ After

The frame is scoped to a plate wrapping only the image, and the caption clears the frame’s downward offset plus its parallax swing.

The same fix resolved an identical latent collision on the Mission chapter’s caption. The frame itself was kept — only its scope changed.
11

Sticky Positioning Fix

Sitewide · Technical

A long-standing CSS rule was silently disabling every sticky element on the site. Found while building the exhibition and finale, which both depend on sticky.

✕ Before

html, body { overflow-x: hidden; }

Makes body its own scroll container, which disables position: sticky for all descendants.

✓ After

html, body { overflow-x: clip; }

Identical clipping behaviour, without creating a scroll container — sticky works again.

Homepage regression-checked afterwards: the brands marquee (its own scroll container) is unaffected, and no page scrolls sideways.
12

Services Page — Complete Rebuild

Services

The Services page was rebuilt from three thin sections into a six-movement editorial experience carrying all client-supplied copy.

✕ Before

  • Static banner with the word “Services”
  • One centred paragraph of generic prose
  • The enquiry form

No vision, offerings or process content.

✓ After

  • Hero — “Design Solutions, Thoughtfully Delivered.”
  • Standfirst + the three audiences
  • The Practice — three tilting 3D plaques
  • What We Offer — dark editorial index
  • Our Process — a stacking deck
  • The Invitation
The existing “Begin a consultation — Tell us about your space” block and its enquiry form were deliberately preserved and now close the page, exactly as the brief required.
13

Services — “What We Offer” Interaction

Services

The first version of this block used an image that chased the cursor. It rendered with visible distortion, so the interaction was replaced rather than patched.

✕ Before

A tilted image plane followed the pointer with spring lag while simultaneously scaling in — forcing constant re-rasterisation, which read as warping and blur.

✓ After

A stationary sticky viewing plate beside the index. Hovering a row crossfades it in place — pure opacity, no movement or rotation.

A small serif caption glides between category names beneath the plate, and the active row stays lit after the cursor leaves, so there is no pop-in / pop-out. Mobile keeps inline framed images per row.
14

Services — Hero Scroll Fade Removed

Services

The hero headline was dimming and re-brightening while scrolling. The scroll-linked opacity was removed so the type holds full strength.

✕ Before

opacity: 1 → 0   (scroll-linked)
y:       0 → 34% (scroll-linked)

✓ After

opacity: 1  (constant)
transform: none

Verified at scroll 0 / 300 / 600px — opacity holds at 1 throughout.

The paired downward drift was removed alongside it: without the fade to mask it, that drift would have pushed the headline into the section’s clip edge and sliced it mid-glyph. The image parallax is retained, so the hero keeps its depth.
15

Custom Cursor — Presence

Sitewide

The branded cursor was easy to lose. Two causes: the logo file carries heavy transparent padding, and the mark is near-black so it vanished on dark photography.

✕ Before

Width 72px — but the mark occupies only ~40% of the file, so the visible circle was ≈28px of hairline stroke. Opacity 0.88, no contrast treatment.

✓ After

Width 96px (116px over links), opacity 1, plus a dual halo: an ivory glow tracing the strokes for dark grounds and a soft dark shadow for weight on light ones.

16

Brands — Origin Tags

Brands

The tag to the right of each partner house in the index was rewritten. All ten replaced with client-supplied descriptors.

✕ Before

✓ After

BrandBeforeAfter
Poltrona FrauItaly · LeatherItaly · Luxury Furniture

17

Brands — Detail Page Copy

Brand detail

The copy shown after clicking into a brand was restored to the client’s exact wording. Several entries had previously been condensed, losing clauses.

✕ Before

“Swiss craftsmanship, iconic leather, extraordinary comfort.”

✓ After

“Swiss craftsmanship. Iconic leather. Extraordinary comfort.”

Taglines and body paragraphs updated for all ten brands. Restorations included dropped clauses in SCIC, Gandia Blasco (“veritable”, “discerning clientele”), Madheke and Neytt.

Three editorial calls, flagged for confirmation: British spellings were kept to match the rest of the site (epitomises, not epitomizes); de Sede retains its lowercase brand styling; and two apparent typos in the source document — “Medheke” and “Crafted Material Solution” — were not propagated.
18

Brands — Flag Correction

Brands

A factual error found while updating the brand data: a Spanish house was displaying an Italian flag.

✕ Before

flagMap[origin] || "/flags/italy.png"

No Spain asset exists, so Gandia Blasco fell through to the Italian flag.

✓ After

flagMap[origin]   // no fallback

Where no matching flag exists, none is shown rather than another country’s.

Action required: supply a spain.png for public/flags/ and the Gandia Blasco card will display correctly.
19

Brands Index — Mobile Tag Layout

Brands

Caught during the responsiveness pass: the new, longer origin tags clipped at the right edge on phones.

✕ Before

Name and tag shared one row at every width. With the tag set shrink-0 beside a wrapping name, “India · Stoneware & Obje…” ran off-screen.

Four tags measured past the viewport edge — worst case 448px on a 390px screen (58px lost).

✓ After

The tag stacks beneath the name on mobile and returns to the same row from tablet up. Names now fit on one line and every tag reads in full.

20

Responsiveness Pass

Sitewide

Every change above was audited at phone (390px) and tablet (768px), measuring real layout geometry rather than eyeballing.

✕ Before

One genuine defect: the Brands index tags overflowing the viewport on mobile (see 19).

✓ After

Fixed and re-verified. Horizontal page overflow measured 0px on Home, About, Services, Brands and Brand-detail — at both widths.

✓ Confirmed responsive

Elements that extend beyond the viewport by design — the brands marquee, the About ticker, the Ken Burns backdrop scale, and the form honeypot — were confirmed intentional and correctly clipped.

Artyk India — internal changelog · generated for review purposes
20 July 2026 · 20 changes across 5 pages
